model_comparison:
Both models performed exceptionally well on this task. The request was for 'normal volume', which effectively asks for a baseline, standard conversational delivery. 

Model 1 delivered a very clean, professional-sounding audio with a neutral tone and consistent standard volume. It is a perfect example of a default reading style.

Model 2 also delivered the text with perfect accuracy and a standard volume. The voice character was slightly different (a bit higher pitched and breathier), but the volume control was strictly 'normal' as requested.

Since both models achieved perfect content accuracy, high naturalness, and strictly adhered to the paralinguistic constraint of 'normal volume' without any deviations like whispering or shouting, they are both rated as fully correct. There is no significant qualitative difference in their adherence to the specific prompt instructions to justify one winning over the other.
